g.data-narration: Introduction to the Concept of Rationality in AI - the foundational concept for intelligent agents
A rational agent acts to maximize expected performance given available information, providing measurable goals
g.text: A rational agent acts to maximize

g.data-narration: Four key components define a rational agent: performance measure, expected outcomes, available information, and percept sequence
Three types: Perfect (unlimited resources), Bounded (limited resources with satisficing), and Computational rationality
The formal definition states that for each percept sequence, a rational agent selects an action maximizing expected performance
g.text: For each possible percept sequence, a rational agent should select an action that is
expected to maximize its performance measure, given the evidence provided by the percept sequence.

g.data-narration: PEAS framework specifies agents by Performance measure, Environment, Actuators, and Sensors - essential for agent design
g.text: Essential for specifying any intelligent agent requirements

g.data-narration: Key environment properties: Observable determines decision complexity, Deterministic affects outcome prediction, Static vs Dynamic affects time pressure
g.text: Full vs Partial - affects decision complexity

g.data-narration: Additional properties: Discrete vs Continuous states, Single vs Multi-agent, Episodic vs Sequential, Known vs Unknown outcomes
Automated Taxi: PEAS includes safety, speed, laws, comfort, profit for performance; city streets for environment; steering for actuators
g.text: Safe driving, fast travel, obey laws,

g.data-narration: Vacuum cleaner agent: Performance is cleaning all dirt quickly; Rational behavior is suck if dirty, move if clean
g.text: If dirty - suck | If clean - move

g.data-narration: Chess agent: Performance is winning; Environment is board and opponent; Uses computational rationality with alpha-beta pruning
Well-designed performance measures lead to desired behavior; Poorly-designed measures cause unintended dangerous behaviors
g.text: Example: Agent maximizing time to goal might take dangerous shortcuts

g.data-narration: Intelligence is ability to learn and adapt like humans; Rationality is acting optimally given goals - they are different concepts
g.text: An agent can be intelligent without being rational, and vice versa

g.data-narration: Key implications: rationality depends on complete percept history, expected not actual performance, uses prior knowledge, is context-dependent
g.text: Important Implications of Rational Agent Definition

g.data-narration: Exam tips: memorize formal definition, know PEAS framework, understand perfect vs bounded rationality, and remember rationality differs from intelligence
Summary: Rationality means best action given info; PEAS framework defines agents; Bounded rationality is practical for real-world applications
g.text: Rationality - Best action given available info | PEAS defines agent | Bounded rationality is practical
Performance measure guides behavior | Rational is not Intelligent | Environment affects design

g.data-narration: Learning complete! Master these concepts to excel in your AI examinations on rational agents
g.text: Master these concepts to excel in your AI examinations
